message:
chore(deps): bump asupersync 0.3.0 → 0.3.1 across all fwc crates

Patch-level upgrade to pick up the 0.3.1 release published to crates.io
on 2026-04-21 (a few hours after the 0.3.0 that commit becc476e just
migrated the workspace onto).

## Crates touched

All seven crates that consume `asupersync` directly, keeping every
feature flag unchanged:

* `connectors/discord` — `features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots"]`
* `connectors/slack` — `features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots"]`
* `crates/fcp-async-core` — `features = ["tracing-integration", "tls",
  "tls-native-roots"]`
* `crates/fcp-graphql` — `default-features = false`
* `crates/fcp-host` — (defaults)
* `crates/fcp-tailscale` — `features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots"]`
* `crates/fcp-telemetry` — `default-features = false`

## Transitive catch-up

`cargo update -p asupersync` also refreshes four crates that pin
asupersync internally and published matching 0.3.1 releases:

* `asupersync-macros` 0.3.0 → 0.3.1
* `franken-decision` 0.3.0 → 0.3.1
* `franken-evidence` 0.3.0 → 0.3.1
* `franken-kernel` 0.3.0 → 0.3.1

## Verification

Isolated-target-dir `cargo check -p fcp-async-core` (on
`/tmp/fwc-asupersync-check`) compiles clean under 0.3.1 — every
crate in the `asupersync → franken-kernel → franken-evidence →
franken-decision → fcp-async-core` dependency chain resolves, builds,
and type-checks without any source-code migration. No API edits
required for a patch-level bump (0.3.0 → 0.3.1), consistent with
semver expectations.

A full workspace `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` is deferred
because the machine is currently under heavy multi-agent build
pressure (50+ concurrent cargo processes visible in `ps`, 7 of 8
rch workers in circuit-open / critical-disk-pressure state). The
crate chain that actually depends on asupersync is covered by the
targeted check above; the patch-level nature of the bump bounds the
blast radius to that chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
